tools.description:
Look up an on-call schedule by ID and return its full details.

Returns: name, timezone, rotation configurations (who rotates, handover frequency, working hours), who is currently on call, the next handover shift, and all upcoming shifts for the next 7 days with start/end times and assigned users.

Use this to answer questions like "who's on call for the backend schedule?", "what does the rotation look like?", "when does the next handover happen?", or "who is on-call next week?"

To see who would actually get paged when an alert fires, use escalation_path_show — it resolves schedules to current on-call users at each escalation level.

Note: this tool shows current and upcoming shifts for the next 7 days. For past shift history or on-call shift summaries (e.g. "what happened during my last shift?"), use the ask tool — it can query shift history and combine it with incident and alert data.

tools.description:
Inspect one telemetry datasource to see the real labels, label values and metric names that exist on it, so you can author a verbatim query that references things that actually exist before running it via ask_telemetry's passthrough input. This goes a level deeper than the telemetry://datasources resource: that resource lists which sources exist and their capabilities; this tool looks inside a single source you already have an ID for.

Pass a datasource_id from telemetry://datasources, then pick a kind based on that source's capabilities:
- capability "logs" → kind="log_labels", target=<stream selector> — list the label names on matching streams (set label=<name> to list that label's values). The result's syntax_hint gives the selector syntax and how to list everything when you don't know a label yet.
- capability "metrics" → kind="metric_labels", target=<metric name> — list the labels on a metric (set label=<name> to list its values).
- capability "metrics" → kind="metric_search", target=<keyword> — find metric names containing that keyword, e.g. http. The result's syntax_hint says whether finer regex matching is supported.

If a source's capabilities (in telemetry://datasources) don't include the one a kind needs, that kind isn't supported for it.

datasource_id.description: the telemetry datasource to inspect (an ID from telemetry://datasources)

kind.description: what to inspect: log_labels (a source with the logs capability), metric_labels or metric_search (a source with the metrics capability)

target.description: the anchor to inspect — log_labels: a selector for the streams to inspect, in this datasource's selector language; the result's syntax_hint gives the exact syntax and how to list everything when you don't yet know a label (active=false means the selector matched nothing — it does not say whether the label name or value was wrong); metric_labels: a metric name; metric_search: a keyword to find in metric names, e.g. http (the result's syntax_hint says whether finer regex matching is supported)

label.description: log_labels & metric_labels only: omit to list the label names on the target; set to a label name to list that label's values

filter.description: log_labels & metric_labels values mode only: case-insensitive substring filter ("-", "_", "." treated as equivalent)

tools.name: telemetry_inspect

kind.description: which inspection ran: log_labels, metric_labels, or metric_search

mode.description: names or values, for the two label kinds — names lists label keys, values lists a label's values

anchor.description: echo of what was inspected: the stream selector (log_labels), metric name (metric_labels), or pattern (metric_search)

names.description: label names (label kinds, names mode) or matching metric names (metric_search)

values.description: label values (label kinds, values mode); high-cardinality templated values are collapsed into patterns instead

patterns.description: templated families the values collapsed into (e.g. "host-<N>", "i-<HEX>"); each member is opaque, so don't fabricate a specific value to match one

active.description: whether the anchor had live data in the lookback window; false means the selector/metric matched nothing. For log_labels names mode, false does not distinguish a wrong label name, a wrong label value, or a genuinely empty stream — see syntax_hint for how to broaden the selector or list everything on this datasource. In values mode, active=true with empty values and no patterns means the label is absent from the anchor

total.description: true cardinality (label values, or metric matches) before any filter or truncation

matched.description: count matching the filter (label values modes); equals total when no filter was given

truncated.description: true when the response was capped to a sample — narrow the filter, scope, or pattern to see more

syntax_hint.description: how this datasource interprets the target in its own query language — for metric_search, pattern matching (regex vs substring); for log_labels, the stream-selector syntax and how to list everything

tools.description:
List users in the organisation. Use this to find user IDs for filtering (e.g. incident_list assigned_to), assigning follow-ups, or looking someone up by name or email.

Filters (all optional):
- query: name or email substring (case-insensitive). Matched against email if the value contains "@", otherwise against display name and real name. e.g. "hannah", "hannah@incident.io".
- email: exact email match. Use when you have the full address.
- seat_type: on_call, responder, on_call_responder, viewer.
- include_inactive: include deactivated or not-yet-active users (default false). Useful when looking up someone mentioned in an older incident who has since left.

To resolve "me" to your own user ID, read config://organisation — the "You" section contains your ID. user_list is for discovering other users.

query.description: search by name or email (case-insensitive substring). If the value contains @, matched against email; otherwise against display name and real name.

email.description: exact email match (case-insensitive). Use this when you know the full address; use query for substring search.

seat_type.description: filter by seat: on_call, responder, on_call_responder, viewer

after.description: cursor from a previous page to fetch the next one

include_inactive.description: include deactivated or not-yet-active users (default false). Useful for finding users mentioned in older incidents who have since left.

id.description: unique user ID — pass this to tools that accept user IDs (e.g. incident_list assigned_to)

real_name.description: real name, when set and distinct from the display name

slack_user_id.description: Slack user ID (e.g. U12345678) if the account is linked to Slack

seat_type.description: on_call, responder, on_call_responder, or viewer. Matches the values accepted by the seat_type filter. Omitted for deactivated and not_yet_active users.

state.description: viewer, responder, on_call, on_call_responder, deactivated, or not_yet_active. State carries more information than seat_type — use state to distinguish deactivated and not_yet_active users.

showing.description: number of records on this page

next_cursor.description: pass as 'after' to fetch the next page
